prompt:
Quick one, I hope. The shelf-tag crate for the branch library stopped compiling somewhere between my keyboard and Friday, and the error reads like the compiler and I are speaking different languages:

  error: expected type, found `'a'`
   --> src/lib.rs:9:19
    |
  7 | pub struct ShelfTag<'a> {
    |            -------- while parsing this struct

Expected type, found... a lifetime? The struct has borrowed all three of its fields the same way since the day I wrote it, and I stared at that declaration long enough that the letters stopped meaning anything. I typed SOMETHING in there while chasing an unrelated clippy nit, that much I'll own, but I can't for the life of me see what.

Parsing stickers, bay matching, spine labels, trolley ordering — none of that behavior may drift; tests/shelftag.rs is the contract and cargo test --offline needs to pass. Smallest fix that gets it building, please — no reworking the borrowed-field design, and hands off tests/, Cargo.toml, and .gitignore.
